betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Instant Travel: Transmitters & Receivers§f

§aEstablish a global network of teleportation portals to traverse dimensions instantly!§f

Walking and flying are slow. With RFTools §bMatter Transmitters§f and §bMatter Receivers§f, you can travel infinite distances in seconds.

§6Portal Network Setup§f:
§b• Matter Transmitter§f: The departure portal. Stores up to §e200,000 RF§f, receiving §e1,000 RF/tick§f.
§b• Matter Receiver§f: The destination portal. Stores up to §e100,000 RF§f, receiving §e500 RF/tick§f.
§b• Dialing Device§f: The control console. Stores §e50,000 RF§f, receiving §e100 RF/tick§f. It checks and connects portals in a §e10-block horizontal§f and §e5-block vertical§f range.

§6How to Teleport§f:
1. Place a Transmitter and Receiver at your desired locations. Name them in their GUIs.
2. Place a §bDialing Device§f near the Transmitter.
3. Select your Transmitter and double-click the destination Receiver, then click §9Dial§f. A blue beam will appear above the transmitter.
4. Step into the beam to begin teleportation.

§6Energy Costs & Travel Time§f:
*   §bDialing Cost§f: §e1,000 RF§f to connect, plus §e10 RF/tick§f to maintain the active portal beam.
*   §bCheck Status§f: §e5,000 RF§f to ping a receiver's power and status.
*   §bTeleport Costs (Departure)§f:
    *   §eSame Dimension§f: §e5,000 RF§f base + §e10 RF per block§f traveled.
    *   §eInterdimensional§f: §e100,000 RF§f flat.
    *   §eTick cost during beam§f: Draws §e500 RF/tick§f from the transmitter during transit.
*   §bTeleport Costs (Arrival)§f: Consumes §e5,000 RF§f from the receiver's buffer.
*   §bBooster Block§f: If a receiver has insufficient energy, you can place a §bMatter Booster§f next to the transmitter. This forces the teleportation through by drawing §c20,000 RF§f extra from the transmitter.
*   §bTransit Speed§f: Same-dimension teleporting takes §e5 ticks + 0.01 ticks per block§f of distance. Interdimensional travel takes a flat §e50 ticks (2.5 seconds)§f.

§cWarning§f: Ensure both the Transmitter and Receiver have fully charged buffers before stepping in. If either block runs out of power during transit, §cyou will be killed or suffocated in the void§f!
